title: Coleslaw
intro: Coleslaw comes from the Dutch word koolsla, meaning “cabbage salad.” Dutch settlers brought the dish to New York in the 1700s, and it eventually became a staple of delicatessens and seafood counters across the Northeast. Classic deli slaw is savory, crisp, and lightly creamy rather than sweet. This version keeps that traditional profile while rebuilding the dressing.

ingredientGroups.title: Cabbage preparation
items: 1/2 medium green cabbage (about 500 g), very thinly shredded

ingredientGroups.title: Vegetables
items: 1/4 small yellow onion, very finely minced

stepGroups.title: Slice the cabbage
items: Quarter the cabbage, remove the core, and slice into very thin ribbons with a sharp knife.
Reserve about 1/2 cup of the freshly shredded cabbage and place it in a covered container in the refrigerator. This portion will be added just before serving to restore fresh crunch.
Place the remaining cabbage in a large bowl and toss with 1 tsp kosher salt.

stepGroups.title: Squeeze the cabbage dry
items: Transfer the salted cabbage to a clean kitchen towel and squeeze firmly over the sink to remove as much liquid as possible.
Return the cabbage to the bowl and fluff it with your fingers so it separates back into loose strands.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the carrot
items: Place the grated carrot in a bowl of cold water and swirl it briefly, about 10 seconds. Drain and squeeze dry in a paper towel or cloth.
This keeps the carrot from bleeding color into the dressing.

stepGroups.title: Combine the vegetables
items: Add the carrot and very finely minced onion to the cabbage and toss to distribute evenly.

stepGroups.title: Make the dressing
items: In a separate bowl whisk together:

stepGroups.title: Dress the slaw
items: Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss thoroughly until everything is evenly coated.

stepGroups.title: Rest the slaw
items: Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. Four hours is better and overnight is excellent.
The cabbage softens slightly and absorbs the dressing.

stepGroups.title: Restore the crunch
items: Just before serving, add the reserved fresh cabbage and toss lightly to combine.
This gives the slaw both marinated cabbage and fresh crisp cabbage at the same time.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Taste and adjust with a little additional pickle juice or black pepper if needed.
